title: Quarterly Access Review Dataset — Interactive Sessions by System

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CIP-004-6 R5.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-004-6 R5 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.13: Quarterly Access Review Dataset — Interactive Sessions by System.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.13' running on index=pam sourcetype=cyberark:ptalog, bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
spl:
index=pam sourcetype=cyberark:ptalog action="Logon" earliest=-90d@d latest=@d
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv address AS address OUTPUT bes_system impact_rating
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| stats earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last dc(address) as targets by user, bes_system, impact_rating
| sort impact_rating, user
description: We assemble quarterly interactive access by user and BES asset to accelerate access certification and attestation.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes pam and sourcetypes cyberark:ptalog, cyberark:ptalog`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Define quarter macros for reproducible windows. (2) Export to GRC certification tool. (3) Restrict index ACLs. (4) Store signed attestation back via HEC if desired. Indexes required: `index=pam`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cyberark:ptalog`, `sourcetype=cyberark:ptalog``; Lookups: `bes_cyber_asset_inventory`, `bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv`.
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, registered change tickets, and approved work can look like noncompliance in raw telemetry; confirm against the current change record before you escalate.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=pam` `sourcetype=cyberark:ptalog`, `bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Define quarter macros for reproducible windows. (2) Export to GRC certification tool. (3) Restrict index ACLs. (4) Store signed attestation back via HEC if desired.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=pam sourcetype=cyberark:ptalog action="Logon" earliest=-90d@d latest=@d
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv address AS address OUTPUT bes_system impact_rating
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| stats earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last dc(address) as targets by user, bes_system, impact_rating
| sort impact_rating, user
```

Understanding this SPL

**Quarterly Access Review Dataset — Interactive Sessions by System (CIP-004-6 R5)** — We assemble quarterly interactive access by user and BES asset to accelerate access certification and attestation.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=pam` `sourcetype=cyberark:ptalog`, `bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: pam; **sourcetype**: cyberark:ptalog. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=pam, sourcetype=cyberark:ptalog, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(bes_system)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user, bes_system, impact_rating** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with CyberArk PVWA / session recording for the same user and time window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (matrix), Pivot (counts), Bar chart (top users)
grandmaExplanation: We assemble quarterly interactive access by user and BES asset to accelerate access certification and attestation. That is important for the big power grid, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.
